exemplar.title: Exploring the impact of mass digitalisation and the extent to which it has impacted the way music is made and listened to in the past 40 years

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Use of headings and some recognizable IntroductionâBodyâConclusion sequence
Figure 15 effectively illustrates comparison of aggregate and physical models
weaknesses: No clear research question stated; aim remains informal
Tables and figures are uncaptioned or mis-numbered, hindering reader orientation
Excessive narrative and digressions disrupt logical flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the report has an adequate overall structure, organizational features are inconsistently applied, transitions are abrupt and much of the narrative is peripheral to the mathematics. These limitations place it in the lower band for Presentation, justifying a score of 1.

strengths: Some consistent use of symbols (e.g. R(t), A, B, D) throughout
Inclusion of multiple representations (graphs, tables, PPF diagram) to support discussion
Explanations are usually linked to models, so the argument is mostly clear
weaknesses: Axes on several figures lack labels and units
Notation is inconsistent (e.g. vector vs. scalar symbols, mis-numbered figures)
Readers must infer intermediate steps; clarity is uneven
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission makes appropriate use of mathematical language and representations, though both are applied inconsistently and with lapses in labelling and notation. Clarity is mostly present but variable, supporting a band 2 score.

strengths: Anecdotal motivation (family story about Fleetwood Mac) personalizes the exploration
First-person narrative shows genuine interest in the topic
weaknesses: Creativity is limited to routine modelling techniques; no novel methods introduced
Personal voice does not significantly drive or deepen the mathematical investigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal engagement is evident in motivation and occasional reflection on hypothesis choices, but initiative and creativity are only minimal. This limited development merits a score of 1.

strengths: Makes some connection between model findings and economic concepts
weaknesses: Critical analysis of errors and limitations is brief and superficial
No quantitative discussion of model validity or error margins
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection goes beyond a token statement but remains at a basic level of critique. The commentary on limitations lacks depth and statistical rigour, placing it in the lowest reflective band for a score of 1.

strengths: Applies course-level mathematics (sine, exponential, piecewise linear) relevantly
Recognizes and justifies switching models when fits fail
Piecewise linear segments accurately capture data trends
weaknesses: Major arithmetic slip in growth-rate notation (0.0932 vs 0.93)
Key modelling choices (phase shift, segment boundaries) lack rigorous justification
No formal regression or combined expression is presented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is appropriate at SL level and generally correct, but noticeable inaccuracies and insufficient analytic justification limit the work to band 2 for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing to abandon an exponential fit in favour of piece-wise linear modelling and openly questioning an initial hypothesis show noticeable independent thinking, although the mathematics itself remains standard.  This aligns with âsignificant evidenceâ (band 2).

The student shows understanding of sine parameters, gradient interpretation and economic context, but explanations are sometimes formulaic and lack rigorous justification (no statistical fit, no domain discussion).  Understanding is therefore âsomeâ, so band 2 is awarded.

Calculations of amplitude, frequency, gradients and growth rates are generally correct, but there are noticeable slips (e.g. K computed as 0.0932 but written later as 0.93; negative revenue scales on combined graphs).  These âmajorâ inaccuracies push accuracy down to band 2 (partially correct).

Large blocks of anecdotal commentary, repeated definitions of variables and long quotations add bulk; conversely some essential mathematical justifications are brief.  Most material is relevant, but unnecessary narrative means the work is only "mostly" concise, fitting band 2.

Basic symbols (R(t), A, B, D) are used, but notation is occasionally incorrect (e.g. âRÎ¸â, inconsistent boldface, mixing t and tâ).  Mathematical sentences sometimes lack appropriate equality signs.  This intermittent accuracy corresponds to âsome appropriate mathematical language and notationâ (band 2).

Explanations usually follow models, yet the reader must occasionally infer steps (e.g. why B = 2Ï/32).  References to figures are sometimes mis-numbered.  Overall clarity is âmostlyâ present but inconsistent, so band 2 is suitable.

The mathematics (piece-wise linear, sine, exponential growth) is squarely at AI SL level and relevant to the aim.  It is applied reasonably effectively to model the data, though not with sophistication.  Therefore band 3 (ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ) suits.

The student includes several graphs, a large data table and hand-drawn PPF diagram.  These representations support the discussion but axes are not always labelled clearly and some figures are blurred.  Use is therefore appropriate but not yet âeffectiveâ, meriting band 2.

The reflection section comments on model suitability, assumptions and external factors, indicating meaningful reflection beyond a token statement.  However, analysis is surface-level and centres largely on model choice rather than deeper insights, placing it in band 2.

While the switch of models shows some initiative, creativity in mathematical approach is limited to routine techniques (sine, linear, exponential).  No original data manipulation or novel method is introduced; thus only âsomeâ creativity is evident (band 1).

The report follows a recognisable introductionâbodyâconclusion sequence and ideas generally progress from data collection to modeling to discussion.  However, transitions are frequently abrupt (e.g. jumping from the sine model to economics without linking mathematics) and some passages digress into lengthy industry background that does not advance the mathematical argument.  Logical development is therefore present but uneven, matching the âsome coherence and logical developmentâ description.

Critical evaluation of errors, limitations or alternative approaches is brief (e.g. acknowledges one incorrect exponential fit, but does not quantify error nor discuss validity statistically).  This demonstrates basic but limited critique, matching band 1.
The student links findings to economic concepts such as elastic demand and PPF, showing a clearâthough not deepâconnection to understanding the broader question.  Hence band 2 is appropriate.

Headings are used and the reader can locate sections, yet sub-sections are not always clearly signposted and several tables/figures are embedded without captions or correct numbering.  The structure is adequate but not consistently clear, so an âadequate organisation with basic structureâ (band 2) is most appropriate.

The topic is personally motivated (parental anecdote) and the narrative is written in first person, guiding the exploration choices.  The personal voice helps drive the investigation but is not deeply integrated into the mathematics, fitting band 2.

annotations.comment: No explicit research question is stated; rephrase the aim as a clear question to focus the exploration and guide the mathematical investigation.
The title is descriptive but overly long; consider refining it to clearly state the research question in concise terms, enhancing reader orientation.
The large table on page 4 lacks a descriptive caption and number; add a caption explaining its content and label it consistently.
Axes on Figure 1 are unlabeled and units are missing; include clear axis titles and units for reader comprehension.
The introductory narrative includes detailed anecdotal context but could be more concise; focus on essential economic background and move peripheral commentary to a later section.
Good personal engagement: the anecdote about parents discovering Fleetwood Mac personalizes the investigation, showing genuine motivation.
The aim is written informally (âI want to analyseâ¦â); restate it as a precise objective or question to enhance clarity and coherence.
Data collection is described without variable definitions or units; specify exactly what each column represents and the units used for revenue and time.
The notation $$\vec R,\vec t$$ and $$\bm D$$ is inconsistent; use standard scalar notation $$R(t)$$, $$t$$ and $$D$$ for clarity.
Amplitude and vertical shift are calculated correctly, but the choice of data points is not justified; explain why those maxima/minima were selected.
Phase shift $$c$$ is set by trial and error without analytical reasoning; consider solving $$B(t-c)=\pi/2$$ at a known peak for rigor.
Caption for Figure 3 on page 6 is minimal; include axis labels, data source and a brief description of what is plotted.
Figure references are out of sequence (mention of figure 5 on page 7 before figure 2); ensure figures are numbered in order of appearance.
Calculation of growth rate $$K$$ is correct, but the later equation uses 0.93 instead of 0.0932, a major slip; correct this to maintain accuracy.
The exponential model fails to pass through \(t=38\) due to asymptote; good recognition to switch models shows thoughtful critique.
Combining digital and physical piecewise functions with the aggregate sine function is innovative, but no formal combined expression is presented; consider deriving one.
The piecewise physical-sales equations model the data points well, showing effective Use of Mathematics for linear trends.
Slope calculations for piecewise linear segments use manual averages rather than regression; state whether these are approximate or computed via least squares.
The discussion on page 10 effectively reflects on hypothesis bias and model choice; this demonstrates genuine personal engagement.
Segmentation into five physicalâsales intervals is descriptive but lacks justification based on data patterns; include rationale from the graph.
The narrative claims insufficient digital data for  transition but appendix shows complete digital series; check consistency between text and data.
Figure 15 effectively illustrates the comparison of aggregate and physical models; good coherence in visual synthesis.
The PPF diagram in Figure 16 lacks axis scales and labels for physical versus digital outputs; define axes and units for economic interpretation.
The economic discussion on page 16 adds context but lacks quantitative linkage to earlier models; integrate revenue cycles with economic events explicitly.
Reflection acknowledges model choice changes and normative assumptions; demonstrates meaningful reflection on methodology.
Figures 11 and 12 have no axis labels or units; add clear labels to communicate which metrics and scales are shown.
The reflection section describes assumptions but lacks critical discussion of limitations (e.g., statistical validity of fits); add critique on error margins.
The final paragraphs offer personal insights on industry changes; this demonstrates clear personal engagement and depth.
Use of external streamingâpayout data lacks integration with revenue model; consider correlating artist payout rates with aggregate trends quantitatively.
